summary.intent: Wire Stokowski's state machine transitions to GStack Claude plugin skills (plan-ceo-review, review, qa, ship, etc.) so that each pipeline stage triggers the appropriate GStack skill, and make this reusable across all projects via an 'extends: gstack' preset.
summary.outcome: Implemented full GStack preset integration in Stokowski: added skill/skip/extends fields to StateConfig, created gstack.yaml preset with 16-state pipeline, added skill-aware runner system prompts with interactive/autonomous modes, created per-gate Linear states for visibility, fixed card flow through pipeline stages, and resolved merge conflicts on CHA-17/CHA-18/CHA-19 branches.

repo: Stokowski is at /Users/samjp/Code/stokowski (not 'stowoksi' as initially mistyped), with workspaces at /Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/
GStack skills are at ~/.claude/skills/gstack/ globally and .claude/skills/gstack/ per-repo; require ./setup to build binary
GStack preamble (update check, session tracking, contributor mode) runs on every skill invocation and is unnecessary overhead in headless Stokowski runs
moltis is a Rust workspace requiring full recompilation even in dev mode (cargo run = cargo build + execute)

All CHA-15 through CHA-19 branches had merge conflicts in crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs because they were all based on pre-CHA-15 main; the pattern was old unfiltered signatures vs. new filtered signatures
Linear 'Human Review' was a catch-all gate state — needed per-gate states (CEO Review — Pending, Eng Review — Pending, etc.) for pipeline visibility
workflow.yaml deep merge: project keys override preset keys entirely for scalar/list values; per-state hooks also fully override rather than merge

code.finding: Added skill (str), skip (bool), skill_mode ('interactive'|'autonomous') to StateConfig; added extends to ServiceConfig; added _deep_merge(), _load_preset(), _apply_skips(), _resolve_skip_target() functions; updated validate_config() to allow agent states with skill alone (no prompt required)

skill_mode='interactive' keeps AskUserQuestion enabled so user stays in loop; skill_mode='autonomous' instructs Claude to auto-pick options and never block; Skill tool must be explicitly added to allowedTools when a state has a skill set
_transition() was hardcoding linear_states.active ('In Progress') for all transitions; fixed to use target state's own linear_state field so cards flow through correct columns
_resolve_current_state() was returning cached state without checking Linear column; fix: check Linear column first on every call and override cache if user manually moved the card
_enter_gate() was hardcoding linear_states.review for all gates; fixed to use gate's own linear_state; _handle_gate_responses() had same hardcoding for both approve and rework paths

Full 16-state GStack pipeline preset: ceo-review(interactive) → gate → eng-review(interactive) → gate → implement → code-review → gate → qa → gate → ship → gate → document-release → retro → done; design-review skipped for moltis (Rust backend, no frontend)
Added Layer 0 skill invocation prefix when state_cfg.skill is set, prepending the skill call to the prompt

workflow: GStack skills are slash commands designed for interactive Claude Code sessions; integrating them into headless orchestration requires suppressing the interactive preamble while preserving the useful system audit step
Per-repo gstack setup: copy ~/.claude/skills/gstack to .claude/skills/gstack, remove .git, commit; run ./setup in after_create hook to build binary
Hook merge limitation: project-level before_run/after_run entirely replaces preset-level hooks rather than appending; safety net hooks should use separate hook types or be documented for inclusion
Rebase is the right tool for fixing merge conflicts on feature branches; gh pr view --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus gives clean merge readiness check
When multiple feature branches all touch the same file and merge sequentially, each subsequent branch needs rebasing after each merge

friction: Initial typo 'stowoksi' vs 'stokowski' caused confusion and wasted search time
GStack preamble runs on every skill invocation causing 'I'll run the preamble and system audit in parallel' noise for every ticket
Stokowski's _issue_current_state cache prevented Linear column overrides from taking effect — had to fix the resolution order
workflow.yaml hook merging is replace-not-append which makes it easy to accidentally lose preset hooks when a project defines its own
The git push said 'Everything up-to-date' for CHA-15 but the branch had never been pushed — confusing because git reported success when the branch was already on remote from a previous push
Cargo rebuild triggered on branch switch even for branches with similar code; large Rust workspace means ~minutes of compile time
Stale conflict marker left in crm_service.rs after rebase edit didn't fully apply — git accepted the rebase commit with the marker in the file
Background task notifications fired 'completed' while cargo was still actively compiling
open_items: CHA-19 (web UI CRM tab) branch rebased and conflict-fixed but not reviewed, merged, or running — user was trying to run it locally when session ended
PR #14 (CHA-17) was ready to merge but user never confirmed — still open
PR #13 (CHA-16) was rebased and ready — user never confirmed merge
PR #12 (CHA-18) was rebased and ready — user never confirmed merge
gstack files copied to moltis repo but not committed to git
moltis workflow.yaml updated to extends:gstack but changes not committed
Consider making hook merging append-by-default rather than replace to prevent accidentally dropping preset hooks
setup-browser-cookies skill not mapped to any Stokowski state — needs a setup step or one-time workflow integration
qa-only and plan-design-review (report-only variants) not exposed as config toggles
/retro state has no gate — user has no opportunity to review the retrospective before it auto-completes
CHA-31 card flow issue was fixed in code but not verified working after restart
